items.title: $7 Million Tourism Project Opens in Duhok as KRG Pushes Investment Drive
items.desc: Built on a 10,000-square-meter site, the project includes a hotel, restaurants, and a swimming pool, adding to Duhok's growing tourism infrastructure.

items.title: Ship Struck Near UAE Coast, UK Maritime Agency Says
items.desc: "A vessel reports being struck by an unknown projectile causing damage to the steering gear. All the crew are reported as safe. There is no environmental impact," the agency said in a statement.

items.title: Trump Vows Iran Will Pay 'Many Times Over' After U.S. Troops Killed in Jordan
items.desc: "Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over!" Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform.

items.title: Lebanese Army Begins Security Deployment in Southern Villages Under US-Backed Framework
items.desc: The US State Department said operations had started in the pilot zones of Froun, Srifa and Zawtar al-Gharbiya "in accordance with the Trilateral Framework and under the auspices of the Military Coordination Group for Lebanon."

items.title: Kurdistan Region President Congratulates UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham, Pledges Stronger Ties
items.desc: “The Kurdistan Region and Iraq have strong and historic ties with the UK, and we look forward to working together to advance our shared values,” Kurdistan Region President Barzani wrote.

items.title: US launches a fresh wave of strikes on Iran
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington/ Tehran The US forces launched a new wave of strikes against Iran, US Central Command (CENTCOM) stated on Monday, adding that the strikes aimed at further degrading military capabilities used to threaten maritime navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. items.title: Transport Ministry reshuffles senior officials at GCPI
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s Transport Minister Wahab Salman Al-Hassani on Monday ordered a broad reshuffle of senior officials at the General Company for Ports of Iraq (GCPI), reassigning and appointing several department heads and port directors, according to an official document. items.title: More than football: 2026 World Cup’s unforgettable stories
items.desc: Shafaq News Spain left North America as world champions, but the 2026 World Cup will also be remembered through a tearful goalkeeper, a disputed cable, a living statue, political intervention, and supporters who turned streets thousands of kilometers apart into football stages. Across 104 matches in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, the expanded tournament produced stories that travelled far

items.title: Iraq locks down Saladin HQ over 50+ corruption warrants
items.desc: Shafaq News- Saladin Iraq’s Federal Commission of Integrity on Monday sealed the Saladin Province headquarters while executing arrest warrants against 55 employees wanted in financial corruption cases, a security source told Shafaq News. No further details were immediately available. An earlier anti-corruption raid in the province on July 6 uncovered about 3 billion Iraqi dinars ($2.3M) in c

items.title: Baghdad records two suicides, probes third death
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Three women died in separate suspected suicide incidents across Baghdad over the past 24 hours, a security source told Shafaq News on Monday. A 24-year-old woman hanged herself from a ceiling fan inside her home, while another died under similar circumstances. Family members and preliminary police information indicated the two women had been suffering from psychological

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
items.desc: The duties take effect within 30 days and mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.desc: The US says the attacks were aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
items.desc: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

items.title: Canadian wildfires rage as Trump warns Carney again over 'poisoning' US air
items.desc: The US president raised the issue with the the Canadian prime minister while the pair attended the World Cup Final in New Jersey.

items.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
items.desc: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

items.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
items.desc: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.

items.title: The war with Iran is escalating. What next?
items.desc: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Suzanne Maloney of the Brookings Institution about the U.S. war with Iran, the increase in hostilities and what it means for the wider region

items.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
items.desc: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

items.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
items.desc: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

items.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
items.desc: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

items.title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader
items.desc: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.

items.desc: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.
